task_completion.description: Select the correct vehicle model in Multiecuscan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan main screen shows 'Fiat Ducato' (specifically 2.3 Multijet) selected in the Make/Model/Version list.

task_completion.description: Inspect the Engine ECU in simulation mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan is connected to the Engine ECU, showing the Parameters tab (e.g., fuel pressure, injection quantity, DPF status) or Errors tab.

task_completion.description: Inspect other required systems (Transmission, ABS, Body Computer, Airbag).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiecuscan is connected to at least one of the other required systems (Gearbox, ABS, Body, or Airbag), showing ECU info, errors, or parameters like wheel speed sensor data or CAN status.

task_completion.description: Create the pre-purchase inspection certificate file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open with the file named 'prepurchase_certificate.txt' saved in the 'MultiecuscanTasks' folder on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Document ECU info, DTCs with classifications, and operational parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains sections for the inspected systems, listing ECU hardware/software versions, DTCs explicitly classified as CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, or CLEARED, and the required operational parameters.

task_completion.description: Include the overall risk score and final purchase verdict.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file explicitly contains an 'overall risk score' (between 0-100) and a final verdict of 'RECOMMENDED', 'CONDITIONAL RECOMMENDED', or 'NOT RECOMMENDED' with a written justification.

integrity.description: Agent used the Multiecuscan GUI to gather data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Multiecuscan application open and being interacted with to read parameters and errors, not just a text editor being filled with fabricated data.

integrity.description: Agent used Simulation mode to connect to ECUs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Multiecuscan connection screens should show 'SIMULATION' (usually in red text in the bottom right or title bar) indicating it is simulating the connection rather than failing to connect to a physical interface.

integrity.description: Agent created the certificate using a text editor or standard file writing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The certificate should be visible in a text editor like Notepad or written via standard OS commands, not just printed to standard output.
